findings.title: FinCEN Designates Swiss MBaer Merchant Bank as Primary Money Laundering Concern
findings.summary: FinCEN proposed rule under Section 311 to sever MBaer Merchant Bank AG from US financial system due to facilitating sanctions evasion and illicit finance. Affects AML pillar via correspondent banking opacity. Actor A4: dark finance infrastructure.
findings.source_name: Money Laundering Watch | Ballard Spahr LLP
findings.claim_excerpt: FinCEN detailed allegations against MBaer on February 26, 2026, proposing measures to ban it from US system.

filters_applied.f2_sanctions_architecture: Swiss bank enables global sanctions circumvention via US correspondent access
filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: Switzerland's commodity-trading hub role exposes regulatory gaps in FINMA oversight

findings.preliminary_assessment: Highlights persistent use of Western correspondent banking by enabler jurisdictions to facilitate illicit flows, signaling need for stricter Section 311 enforcement.
findings.confidence_basis: Direct FinCEN NPRM details corroborated across enforcement reporting

sources_cited.excerpt: MBaer designated primary money laundering concern by FinCEN Feb 2026
Section 311 action against MBaer for sanctions evasion

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Primary regulatory action with clear USG attribution; multi-source confirmation on details.

findings.episodic_reason: Novel Section 311 designation targeting Swiss bank

cross_monitor_relevance.gmm: Sanctions regime gap via correspondent banking

findings.review_reason: Assess impact on Swiss-US banking ties and FINMA response

findings.title: FinCEN and OFAC Target Huione Group as Southeast Asia Scam Conduit
findings.summary: FinCEN final rule and OFAC sanctions sever Huione Group from US system for laundering scam proceeds via TCO networks. Impacts AML/CTF via digital-asset and professional enablers. Actor A5/A2.
findings.claim_excerpt: FinCEN issued final rule severing Huione Group; OFAC designated as TCO for scam laundering.

filters_applied.f2_sanctions_architecture: Targets scam-to-TCO pipeline overlapping SE Asia networks
filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: US enforcement exposes gaps in Singapore/HK wealth mgmt compliance

findings.preliminary_assessment: Reinforces US strategy against crypto-enabled scam finance, but highlights enabler jurisdiction reliance on USG actions.
findings.confidence_basis: Coordinated FinCEN/OFAC actions detailed in legal analysis

sources_cited.excerpt: Oct 2025 FinCEN rule on Huione Group TCO
Sanctions on Huione for cybercrime facilitation

cross_monitor_relevance.gmm: Sanctions on SE Asia illicit finance

findings.review_reason: Monitor SE Asia scam network adaptations post-sanctions

findings.title: EU Advances 6AMLD Implementation for Harmonized AML Enforcement
findings.summary: EU's Sixth AML Directive enhances cross-member state transparency and prosecution of laundering. Targets AML pillar, professional enablers in fund domiciliation. Actor A2.
findings.claim_excerpt: Sixth AML Directive (6AMLD) harmonizes AML regulations across EU member states.

filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: Luxembourg fund structures face heightened BO scrutiny

findings.preliminary_assessment: Strengthens EU enabler jurisdiction controls but enforcement gaps persist in Luxembourg holdings.
findings.confidence_basis: Ongoing EU regulatory process corroborated by multiple AML overviews

sources_cited.excerpt: EU AMLD updates in mutual evaluations

findings.title: FATF Releases Comprehensive Terrorist Financing Risks Update
findings.summary: FATF webinar and report detail evolving TF methods, online tactics, and future trends since 2015. Covers CTF pillar across jurisdictions. Actor A6.
findings.claim_excerpt: FATF report provides comprehensive overview of terrorist financing risks, first since 2015.

filters_applied.f4_conflict_finance: Online and combined methods fund non-state groups

findings.preliminary_assessment: Signals shift to digital TF channels, underscoring gaps in Tier A jurisdiction monitoring.

sources_cited.excerpt: Comprehensive Update on Terrorist Financing Risks

findings.episodic_reason: First major FATF TF update in decade
findings.review_reason: Apply to Tier A CTF assessments

findings.title: FinCEN Expands GTO for Southwest Border Cash Transactions
findings.summary: FinCEN enhances Geographic Targeting Orders for cash-heavy areas tied to drug cartels. AML focus on TBML networks. Actor A3.
findings.claim_excerpt: FinCEN’s expanded GTO disrupts money laundering tied to drug cartels along Southwest border.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Addresses TBML volume in US-Mexico corridor, but professional enablers remain under-scrutinized.
findings.confidence_basis: Single detailed enforcement report; awaits FinCEN primary confirmation

sources_cited.excerpt: Expanded GTO for border cash transactions

findings.title: Tornado Cash Developer Roman Storm Trial Highlights Crypto Sanctions Risks
findings.summary: DOJ trial of Roman Storm underscores shift to knowledge-based AML charges for mixers. Affects CPF/AML via digital assets. Actor A5.
findings.claim_excerpt: Roman Storm trial on money laundering, unlicensed MSB, sanctions violations for Tornado Cash.

filters_applied.f3_enabler_jurisdiction: US prioritizes national security in crypto enforcement

findings.preliminary_assessment: DOJ pivot signals architecture targeting DeFi for proliferation risks.
findings.confidence_basis: Legal analysis of ongoing DOJ case

jurisdiction_summary.uk: No new OFSI actions; monitoring Companies House BO opacity.
jurisdiction_summary.us: Multiple FinCEN/OFAC enforcements target banks, crypto, scams.
jurisdiction_summary.uae: No qualifying events in scan window.
jurisdiction_summary.russia: Ongoing dark fleet signals absent today.

coverage_notes.under_coverage_correction_applied: Central Asia (Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan) for Russian rerouting; no signals
